danger_signals.visual_form: Sound effect / likely enemy sprite animation
danger_signals.meaning: Lethal threat — player character is 'brutally killed' by dog barks

danger_signals.visual_form: Meter UI element that depletes during gameplay
danger_signals.meaning: Resource loss — indicates vulnerability state or hunger mechanic

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Dogs (as lethal hostile entities in US version; part of original Q-Tarō licensed IP in Japan)

world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player movement/action constrained as meter depletes; death triggered by dog encounters when vulnerable

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Score notification display (with regional text variants)
Dark US version vs. lively Japanese version; logo replaced with hearts in US; character redesigns

environmental_storytelling.example: Q-Tarō (licensed hungry ghost) redrawn as 'Chubby Cherub' (original property angel cherub) to obscure unknown Japanese IP from Western audiences
Dark US title screen vs. lively Japanese version establishes different tonal contexts for the same mechanical game
Q logo replaced with hearts in US version — shifts identity from anime-licensed property to generic platformer
Power meter depletes faster in Wan-Wan Panic (JP) than Chubby Cherub (US), signaling different difficulty/urgency narratives

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Bark attacks that instantly kill player character — primary threat mechanic
Power meter depletion reflects hunger state; vulnerability increases as meter empties

notes: This is a minimal-documentation T3 entry. The game's manifestation layer is heavily shaped by regional localization: the JP version communicates 'licensed anime property' through visual identity (lively title screen, Q logo, original characters), while the US version removes all anime signaling through darkness, heart symbols, and character redesigns. Danger is communicated purely through mechanical threat (dog barks = instant death) rather than visual warning systems. The power meter is the primary world-state indicator, visually communicating player vulnerability progression. No faction politics or complex social systems are documented — the game operates on a simple predator-prey communication model.
sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Chubby Cherub article)
